feature_name: Network Security Group Support
notes: KV not subnet-injected; proxy via network ACL default-deny or public_network_access=Disabled

feature_name: Virtual Network Integration
notes: VNet service endpoint rules or private endpoints as VNet integration signal

feature_name: Azure Private Link
notes: Checks for at least one Approved private endpoint connection embedded in vault.properties

feature_name: Disable Public Network Access
notes: PASS if public_network_access=Disabled OR default_action=Deny

feature_name: Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data
notes: Network ACLs restrict exfil surface; ARM-checkable DLP proxy. Defender for KV (LT-1) covers anomaly detection separately.

feature_name: Encrypt Data in Transit
notes: minimum_tls_version in (1.2 / TLS1_2 / 1.3 / TLS1_3) → PASS

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Platform-Managed Keys
notes: Standard or Premium SKU confirms Microsoft-managed platform key backing

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Customer-Managed Key
notes: KV manages CMKs for other services; KV service metadata encrypted with MS-managed HSMs — customer CMK for KV-at-rest not supported

feature_name: Manage Cryptographic Keys using Key Management Service
notes: KV IS the key management service; control inherently satisfied — auto-PASS

feature_name: Certificate Management using Azure Key Vault
notes: KV IS the certificate management service; control inherently satisfied — auto-PASS

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — Disable Local Auth
notes: enable_rbac_authorization=True disables legacy vault access policies (local auth methods); GA 2020

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — AAD Auth Required
notes: enable_rbac_authorization=True enforces Entra ID as sole auth method for data plane

feature_name: Use Azure AD Managed Identities for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: RBAC proxy — enable_rbac_authorization=True confirms MI assignments are supported; full enum needs azure-mgmt-authorization

feature_name: Use Azure AD Service Principals for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: RBAC proxy — enable_rbac_authorization=True confirms SP assignments are supported; full enum needs azure-mgmt-authorization

feature_name: Restrict Resource Access Based on Conditions — Conditional Access
notes: CA policies are Entra tenant-level configuration; not readable via ARM; always UNKNOWN

feature_name: Restrict the Exposure of Credential and Secrets — Use Azure Key Vault
notes: KV IS the secrets management service; control inherently satisfied — auto-PASS

feature_name: Enable Threat Detection Capabilities — Microsoft Defender for Key Vault
notes: Real Defender check via azure-mgmt-security; pricing_tier=Standard → PASS. Unlike Redis which has no Defender product.

feature_name: Enable Logging for Azure Resources
notes: MonitorManagementClient.diagnostic_settings.list(vault.id); any enabled log category → PASS

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Azure Backup Service
notes: Azure Backup does not support standard KV; per-object CLI backup exists for Managed HSM but not ARM-checkable

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Native Soft-Delete and Purge Protection
notes: Both enable_soft_delete=True AND enable_purge_protection=True required → PASS

feature_name: Use Only Approved Azure Services — Azure Policy Support
notes: Tags presence as proxy for policy governance; empty tags → FAIL

feature_name: Use Only Approved Applications in Virtual Machine — Defender for Cloud AAC
notes: PaaS — Adaptive Application Controls target VMs and Arc servers only

feature_name: Separate and Limit Highly Privileged/Administrative Users — Disable Local Admin
notes: enable_rbac_authorization=False = legacy vault policies active = broad permissions without RBAC granularity = local-admin equivalent

feature_name: Follow Just Enough Administration Principle — RBAC for Data Plane
notes: enable_rbac_authorization=True = data-plane RBAC active; full JEA verification requires azure-mgmt-authorization

feature_name: Determine Access Process for Microsoft Support — Customer Lockbox
notes: KV in Lockbox supported services list (GA ~2020); enablement is subscription-level — per-vault ARM check not available; always UNKNOWN with rationale

feature_name: EDR Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer-accessible compute; EDR agent deployment not supported

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer OS layer; anti-malware not applicable

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution Health Monitoring
notes: PaaS — ES-2 not applicable therefore ES-3 not applicable

feature_name: Azure Automation State Configuration
notes: PaaS — Automation DSC targets VMs and Arc machines; no customer OS layer

feature_name: Azure Policy Guest Configuration Agent
notes: PaaS — Guest Config agent requires customer-accessible guest OS

feature_name: Custom Container Images
notes: PaaS — Microsoft manages KV runtime; no customer container images

feature_name: Custom VM Images
notes: PaaS — no customer-supplied VM images accepted by Key Vault

feature_name: Vulnerability Assessment using Microsoft Defender
notes: Defender for KV provides threat detection not VA; no software stack to scan on PaaS

feature_name: Azure Automation Update Management
notes: PaaS — Microsoft patches KV infrastructure; Update Management targets VMs only
